annotations.label: entity
annotations.definition: An entity is anything that exists or has existed or will exist. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [001-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: continuant
annotations.definition: A continuant is an entity that persists, endures, or continues to exist through time while maintaining its identity. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [008-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: occurrent
annotations.definition: An occurrent is an entity that unfolds itself in time or it is the instantaneous boundary of such an entity (for example a beginning or an ending) or it is a temporal or spatiotemporal region which such an entity occupies_temporal_region or occupies_spatiotemporal_region. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [077-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: independent continuant
annotations.definition: b is an independent continuant = Def. b is a continuant which is such that there is no c and no t such that b s-depends_on c at t. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [017-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: spatial region
annotations.definition: A spatial region is a continuant entity that is a continuant_part_of spaceR as defined relative to some frame R. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [035-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: temporal region
annotations.definition: A temporal region is an occurrent entity that is part of time as defined relative to some reference frame. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [100-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: two-dimensional spatial region
annotations.definition: A two-dimensional spatial region is a spatial region that is of two dimensions. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [039-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: spatiotemporal region
annotations.definition: A spatiotemporal region is an occurrent entity that is part of spacetime. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [095-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: process
annotations.definition: p is a process = Def. p is an occurrent that has temporal proper parts and for some time t, p s-depends_on some material entity at t. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [083-003])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: disposition
annotations.definition: b is a disposition means: b is a realizable entity & b’s bearer is some material entity & b is such that if it ceases to exist, then its bearer is physically changed, & b’s realization occurs when and because this bearer is in some special physical circumstances, & this realization occurs in virtue of the bearer’s physical make-up. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [062-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: realizable entity
annotations.definition: To say that b is a realizable entity is to say that b is a specifically dependent continuant that inheres in some independent continuant which is not a spatial region and is of a type instances of which are realized in processes of a correlated type. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [058-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: zero-dimensional spatial region
annotations.definition: A zero-dimensional spatial region is a point in space. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [037-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: quality
annotations.definition: a quality is a specifically dependent continuant that, in contrast to roles and dispositions, does not require any further process in order to be realized. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [055-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: specifically dependent continuant
annotations.definition: b is a specifically dependent continuant = Def. b is a continuant & there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region and which is such that b s-depends_on c at every time t during the course of b’s existence. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [050-003])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: role
annotations.definition: b is a role means: b is a realizable entity & b exists because there is some single bearer that is in some special physical, social, or institutional set of circumstances in which this bearer does not have to be& b is not such that, if it ceases to exist, then the physical make-up of the bearer is thereby changed. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [061-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: fiat object part
annotations.definition: b is a fiat object part = Def. b is a material entity which is such that for all times t, if b exists at t then there is some object c such that b proper continuant_part of  c at t and c is demarcated from the remainder of c by a two-dimensional continuant fiat boundary. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [027-004])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: one-dimensional spatial region
annotations.definition: A one-dimensional spatial region is a line or aggregate of lines stretching from one point in space to another. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [038-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: object aggregate
annotations.definition: b is an object aggregate means: b is a material entity consisting exactly of a plurality of objects as member_parts at all times at which b exists. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [025-004])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: three-dimensional spatial region
annotations.definition: A three-dimensional spatial region is a spatial region that is of three dimensions. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [040-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: site
annotations.definition: b is a site means: b is a three-dimensional immaterial entity that is (partially or wholly) bounded by a material entity or it is a three-dimensional immaterial part thereof. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [034-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: object
annotations.definition: b is an object means: b is a material entity which manifests causal unity of one or other of the types CUn listed above & is of a type (a material universal) instances of which are maximal relative to this criterion of causal unity. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [024-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: generically dependent continuant
annotations.definition: b is a generically dependent continuant = Def. b is a continuant that g-depends_on one or more other entities. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [074-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: function
annotations.definition: A function is a disposition that exists in virtue of the bearer’s physical make-up and this physical make-up is something the bearer possesses because it came into being, either through evolution (in the case of natural biological entities) or through intentional design (in the case of artifacts), in order to realize processes of a certain sort. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [064-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: process boundary
annotations.definition: p is a process boundary =Def. p is a temporal part of a process & p has no proper temporal parts. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [084-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: one-dimensional temporal region
annotations.definition: A one-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is extended. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [103-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: material entity
annotations.definition: A material entity is an independent continuant that has some portion of matter as proper or improper continuant part. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [019-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: continuant fiat boundary
annotations.definition: b is a continuant fiat boundary = Def. b is an immaterial entity that is of zero, one or two dimensions and does not include a spatial region as part. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [029-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)
Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: one-dimensional continuant fiat boundary
annotations.definition: a one-dimensional continuant fiat boundary is a continuous fiat line whose location is defined in relation to some material entity. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [032-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: process profile
annotations.definition: b is a process_profile =Def. there is some process c such that b process_profile_of c (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [093-002])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: relational quality
annotations.definition: b is a relational quality = Def. for some independent continuants c, d and for some time t: b quality_of c at t & b quality_of d at t. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [057-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: two-dimensional continuant fiat boundary
annotations.definition: a two-dimensional continuant fiat boundary (surface) is a self-connected fiat surface whose location is defined in relation to some material entity. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [033-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: zero-dimensional continuant fiat boundary
annotations.definition: a zero-dimensional continuant fiat boundary is a fiat point whose location is defined in relation to some material entity. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [031-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: zero-dimensional temporal region
annotations.definition: A zero-dimensional temporal region is a temporal region that is without extent. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [102-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)

annotations.label: history
annotations.definition: A history is a process that is the sum of the totality of processes taking place in the spatiotemporal region occupied by a material entity or site, including processes on the surface of the entity or within the cavities to which it serves as host. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [138-001])

provenance.source: Basic Formal Ontology (OBO Foundry canonical serialization)
